beats.narration_text: G'day. This is Liam, in for Bear. One Claude prompt. A live interactive simulation of 4×4 matrix heatmap.
Quantum mechanics is full of non-obvious results. Today's: 4×4 matrix heatmap. Key checkable number: 1.5876e-4 eV at 10⁶ V/m. Let us build the demonstration.

props.prompt: Build me an interactive D3 simulation of 4×4 matrix heatmap. Use D3 version 7 from CDN. Single-file HTML, all physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Add at least one slider or control. Teardown palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. D3 version 7. All the physics runs in the browser. No server. No build step.
Here is the baseline — the simulation at rest. 4×4 matrix heatmap.
Now interact with the control — drag the slider, click the toggle. Watch the physics respond.
The simulation updates in real time. 1.5876e-4 eV at 10⁶ V/m. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live quantum mechanics demonstration. 4×4 matrix heatmap. Bear reviews these in the morning and ships the ones that pass.
Your turn. Extend this 09 hydrogen stark effect simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.

props.prompt: Extend this 09 hydrogen stark effect simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.
